labels.reason: The expert already knows embedding storage and nearest-neighbor retrieval.
The basic RAG pipeline is known background and needs no extra guidance.
This connects familiar indexing workflows to the new freshness-budget idea.
The quantified stale-answer improvement is a new decision-relevant result.
The write-amplification result is a second genuinely new quantitative claim.
Known drift monitoring is connected to selective reindexing actions.
Repeated deployment checklists are explicitly outside the reader's interests.
Vendor pricing is explicitly irrelevant to the reader's technical goal.
